specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward in a cross pattern
specs.notes: TTY bolts must be replaced. Final stage is 90 degrees additional rotation. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in sequence from center outward in cross pattern
specs.notes: Use proper tightening sequence per service manual
Apply anti-seize to prevent seizure from heat cycling

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in cross pattern starting from center
specs.notes: Do not overtighten to avoid oil pan warpage. Use new gasket.

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in sequence from center outward
specs.notes: Do not overtighten to avoid warping cover

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in sequence per service manual
specs.notes: TTY bolts must be replaced. Final stage is additional 90 degrees. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
TTY bolts must be replaced. Final stage is additional 90 degrees. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Do not overtighten. Use torque wrench for proper installation.
Use thread locker. Hold flywheel/flexplate when tightening.
Various bolt lengths - ensure proper bolt placement. Apply RTV to bolts entering water jacket.
Always tighten in star pattern. Re-torque after 50 miles.
Critical safety component - verify torque specification with service manual
Use only high temperature brake grease on slide pins
Always use new copper washers. Bleed brakes after installation.
Ensure ball joint stud is properly seated before tightening
Install new cotter pin. Align for cotter pin hole if needed.
Vehicle should be at ride height when final torquing. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Hold strut rod to prevent spinning when tightening
Do not overtighten to avoid crushing bushings
Apply thread locker. Tighten in cross pattern.
Install new cotter pin or stake nut as applicable

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in cross pattern from center outward
specs.notes: Do not overtighten to avoid pan warpage. Use new gasket.
Use new washer/gasket if equipped
Apply thread locker. Mark driveshaft position before removal.
Various bolt lengths - ensure proper bolt placement. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Use new washer/gasket. Do not overtighten to avoid oil pan threads damage.

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward in a crisscross pattern
specs.notes: TTY bolts - replace with new bolts. Final stage is additional 90 degrees from stage 3 position.

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward in a spiral pattern
specs.notes: Tighten in 2-3 passes to final specification

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward
specs.notes: Use anti-seize to prevent galling. Check for warpage if replacing.

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward in a crisscross pattern
specs.notes: Do not overtighten. Replace gasket/RTV as needed.

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward
specs.notes: Replace rubber gasket. Do not overtighten to avoid gasket damage.

specs.lubrication: Light engine oil on threads and bearing surfaces
specs.notes: TTY bolts - replace with new. Verify main bearing clearances before assembly.
TTY bolts - replace with new. Check rod bearing clearance before assembly.
Do not overtighten. Use torque wrench for final tightening.
Use flywheel holding tool. Apply thread locker to bolts.

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward
specs.notes: Replace gasket/RTV sealant. Various bolt lengths - note positions during removal.
Retorque after 50-100 miles. Check torque periodically.
Apply thread locker. Critical safety component - verify torque.
Lubricate pins with brake grease. Check pin movement after installation.
Replace copper washers. Bleed brake system after installation.
Critical steering component. Perform alignment after replacement.
Install new cotter pin. Alignment required after replacement.
Tighten with vehicle at ride height. Alignment required after service.
Use strut spring compressor. Hold strut rod when tightening.
Replace if rubber bushings are damaged. Hold ball stud when tightening.
Apply thread locker. Critical safety component - verify torque.
One-time use nut. Stake or install new cotter pin as applicable.

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward in crisscross pattern
specs.notes: Replace gasket or RTV. Do not overtighten to avoid pan warpage.
Replace sealing washer. Do not overtighten aluminum oil pan.
Apply thread locker. Mark driveshaft position before removal.
Various bolt lengths - note positions. Apply thread locker to bolts.
Replace sealing washer. Do not overtighten aluminum oil pan.

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward in a spiral pattern
specs.notes: TTY bolts must be replaced. Never reuse. Final torque plus angle method required.

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward in cross pattern
specs.notes: Ensure intake manifold gaskets are properly seated before tightening

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward
specs.notes: Use anti-seize on threads to prevent seizure due to heat cycling

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in cross pattern from center outward
specs.notes: Do not overtighten as oil pan is aluminum and can crack

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward
specs.notes: Convert to inch-lbs: 70 in-lbs. Do not overtighten to prevent gasket damage

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten caps in sequence 1-2-3-4-5 for each stage
specs.lubrication: Light engine oil on threads and under bolt head
specs.notes: TTY bolts must be replaced. Critical engine component - verify with OEM service manual for this specific application

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten both bolts evenly in stages
specs.notes: TTY bolts must be replaced. Ensure proper rod cap orientation before tightening
Do not overtighten. Thread spark plugs in by hand first to avoid cross-threading
Use proper flywheel holding tool to prevent crankshaft rotation during tightening
Various bolt lengths used - ensure correct bolt placement during reassembly
Always retorque after first 50 miles when installing new wheels
Critical safety component. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application

specs.lubrication: Brake caliper grease on pin and boot
specs.notes: Ensure slide pins move freely after installation and proper boot seating
Always replace copper washers/seals. Do not overtighten to prevent hose damage
Ensure ball joint stud is properly seated in knuckle before tightening
Install new cotter pin after tightening. May need to tighten slightly to align cotter pin hole

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten with suspension at ride height
specs.notes: Must be tightened at normal ride height to prevent bushing damage

specs.torque_sequence: Hold strut shaft while tightening nut
specs.notes: Use proper strut shaft holding tool to prevent shaft rotation
Do not overtighten as this can damage the ball joint boot
Ensure hub is fully seated against knuckle before tightening bolts
One-time use nut. Always replace with new nut. Stake or use cotter pin as specified

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in cross pattern from center outward
specs.notes: Do not overtighten. Pan is typically aluminum or thin steel and can distort easily
Replace drain plug gasket/washer each time. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application
Mark driveshaft position before removal to maintain balance
Various bolt lengths used. Ensure proper bolt placement and thread engagement
Replace drain plug gasket each oil change. Do not overtighten aluminum oil pan
